content.rendered:
[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space height=”50px” el_class=”height30″][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row el_class=”flexrow”][vc_column width=”1/4″ el_class=”flextwo gt-custom-sidebar-column”][vc_widget_sidebar sidebar_id=”honeymoon-package-international-sidebar”][/vc_column][vc_column width=”3/4″ el_class=”second-div-text flexone”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Millions of tourists visit Greece every year to enjoy the pleasant weather, breathtaking landscape, and vibrant culture. But most importantly, Greece is a budget-friendly destination! At ATW Travels, we offer a range of Greece honeymoon packages that are affordable yet include the very best that the country offers.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Why Visit Greece?” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Greece is beautiful and has a vibrant history that dates back to the Bronze Age and was once the seat of an ancient civilization. Everything in Greece has an irresistible charm: the black sand beaches in Santorini, the all-white architecture in Oia and Crete, or super luxurious resorts in Mykonos.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26285″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”When is the Best Time to Visit Greece?” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

April to June is the spring season in Greece and the best time to visit the country. November through March is off-season and the best time for discounted Greece honeymoon packages. Early autumn, i.e., September and October are equally pleasant and ideal for a vacation. They’re also the best time to find great deals on Greece honeymoon packages. However, July and August are peak tourist seasons and not the perfect time for a laidback romantic vacation. Not to mention, almost everyone in Greece is on vacation in August.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”How to Get to and Around Greece?” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Athens is the country’s capital city with an international airport and a rail station that is well connected with the rest of the world. The Eleftherios Venizelos International Airport in Athens is the primary airport with frequent flights across all destinations. While you cannot get to Athens directly from Europe, some trains connect Thessaloniki to Sofia, Bucharest, and Belgrade. You can then take the suburban railway to get to Athens by train.

If you’re planning to visit Greece by boat, consider booking a cruise from India, although we don’t offer one currently.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Top Destinations You Must Add to Your Romantic Greece Honeymoon Tour Package” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Athens” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Athens is the best place to understand Greek history and architecture. You may take a walk down the lanes of history, admire the ruins and remnants, and marvel at some of the beautifully preserved cultures. The National Garden, Temple of Zeus, The Acropolis, and The Parthenon are some of Athens’s most famous attractions.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26286″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Santorini” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Santorini is a part of the Cyclades – a group of 220 Greek islands that once had an outbreak of cholera. The walls were painted white using limestone as they had antibacterial properties. The blue colour is obtained from Loulaki, a cleaning agent commonly found across Greece. Today, the blue and white landscape has become a significant tourist attraction. The island is also famous for its gorgeous old churches, picturesque bistros, and amazing sunsets. No wonder it is an integral part of every romantic Greece honeymoon tour package.

Some of the other prominent attractions in Santorini include:

  • Prehistoric Thera Museum
  • Santo Winery
  • Perissa Beach
  • Red Beach
  • Cable Ride from Old Port to Fira
  • The Black Pebble Kamari beach

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Corfu Island” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Corfu is irresistible with golden sun-kissed beaches, clear waters, volcanic mountains, and a breathtaking ambiance. This is a true nest for love birds and a perfect honeymoon spot. You could go hiking, scuba diving, or walk along the cobbled streets with your loved one. Remember to include Corfu in your Greece honeymoon package itinerary when planning your tour.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Mykonos” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

If you are a couple looking to explore Greece’s nightlife, remember to include Mykonos in your Greece honeymoon package. The place is full of amazing pubs and discotheques where you can party the night away with your loved one. Other attractions include beautiful secluded beaches, the Aegean Maritime Museum, gondola rides at Little Venice, and Mykonos Windmills.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Spetses” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Spetses looks straight out of a Bollywood movie and is perfect for a romantic vacation. From arriving on a ferry to the horsecart ride to go around town, everything about Spetses is larger than life.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Rhodes Island” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

The golden sands and turquoise waters of Rhodes Island beckon lovers from across the world. The Palace of the Grand Master of Knights, Elli Island, Valley of Butterflies, and the Lindos Acropolis are popular spots to spend time with your loved ones.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26288″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Don’t Forget to Add These Activities in Your Greece Honeymoon Packages” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

There are so many activities that make your vacation extra special. We’ve listed some for you below:

  • Touring Firo and Oia with their amazingly designed homes and bright peaks of bougainvillea trees adorning them.
  • Experiencing the beautiful cliffs and sunsets in Greece.
  • Swimming at Palea Kameni, the famous hot water springs in Greece.
  • Taking a yacht ride on the blue oceans across the islets of Greece.
  • Visiting Naxos Island to experience water rides and scuba diving.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner full_width=”stretch_row_content_no_spaces” css=”.vc_custom_1645675083319{margin-bottom: 20px !important;padding-top: 40px !important;padding-bottom: 40px !important;background-color: #1a2b48 !important;}” el_id=”mobileshoecontact” el_class=”show-mobile”][vc_column_inner el_class=”whitecontent” width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1646220294427{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner el_class=”whitecontent” width=”1/2″ css=”.vc_custom_1644654313324{border-left-width: 1px !important;border-left-color: #ffffff !important;border-left-style: solid !important;}”][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1653552526259{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

Book Your Dream Vacay Today!

[/vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Where to Stay in Greece” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

At ATW Travels, we’ve partnered with some of the best hotels and resorts across Greece to provide the best accommodation for our guests. Some of the best hotels to stay in Greece include:

  • Csky Hotel, Santorini
  • Adamant Suites, Santorini
  • Kirini Suites And Spa, Santorini
  • Corfu Imperial, Corfu
  • Grand Resort Lagonissi, Athens

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Shopping Destinations Included in Most Greece Honeymoon Packages” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

There’s no better place than Greece for shopaholics. There are hundreds of flea markets and high street stores in and around major tourist attractions: Some of the major shopping destinations include:

  • Piraeus Flea Market for Bags and Shoes
  • Ermou Street for fast fashion
  • Central Market to buy the best cheese in Greece
  • Kifisia to buy the best luxury brands
  • Aeolou and Agios Markos Streets for silk, cotton, and linen fabric
  • The Plaka for jewelry, antiques, and souvenirs
  • Avissinias Square for carpets, antiques, and vinyl
  • Glyfada to buy local high street brands
  • Monastiraki Flea Market for jewelry and antiques

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_single_image image=”26292″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”What to Eat When in Greece” use_theme_fonts=”yes”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]Don’t miss out on these local delicacies in Greece:

  • Taramasalata – a fish roe dip mixed with olive oil and lemon juice and placed on a bed of some starch like bread, potato, or almonds.
  • Local olives
  • Dolmades- vegetables stuffed with a combination of rice and meat or vegetables along with a heady concoction of exotic herbs.
  • Moussaka- A local baked delicacy of sautéed aubergine, minced lamb, puréed tomato, onion, garlic, and spices, and a topping of béchamel sauce and cheese.
  • Varieties of cheeses
  • Desserts – baklawas, Greek yoghurt, galaktoboureko, semolina halva, & more.

Greece is a dream come true destination, especially if you love art, architecture, culture, and the Mediterranean climate. We promise to make your honeymoon the best vacation of your life. What are you waiting for! Call us to choose from the best Greece honeymoon tour packages. Get to know about our daily offers by visiting our Instagram Profile.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner el_class=”removepadding”][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]
